docs.text:
• Code Link
  • Designer > Code Link

docs.title: elysium.commands
docs.text: App-level undo/redo and actions — Command/FunctionCommand/MacroCommand, an UndoStack, and Action (Qt's QUndoStack/QUndoCommand/QAction).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
App-level undo/redo + actions — Qt's QUndoStack / QUndoCommand / QAction.

A :class:Command is a reversible operation (redo applies it, undo reverts it). An :class:UndoStack records executed commands and walks them back and forth, with optional merging (coalescing consecutive edits — e.g. typing into one undo step), macros (grouping several commands into one), a command limit, and a clean marker (the saved-state index, for a "modified ●" indicator).

An :class:Action is a single trigger — text + shortcut + enabled/checked state + a callback — that a menu item, a toolbar button, and a keyboard shortcut all share, so they never drift out of sync.

docs.title: Command dataclass
docs.text: A reversible operation. redo() applies it (also called the first time, when pushed); undo() reverts it. Override both. For commands that should coalesce (e.g. successive keystrokes), give them a non-negative :attr:merge_id and implement :meth:merge_with.

docs.title: merge_with(other: 'Command') -> bool
docs.text: Fold other into self (both already executed). Return True if merged — the stack then drops other. Default: no merging.

docs.title: FunctionCommand dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Command

A command from two callables — the quick way to make an undoable edit.

docs.title: MacroCommand dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Command

A composite of child commands applied/reverted as one undo step.

docs.title: UndoStack dataclass
docs.text: Records commands and undoes/redoes them. index is the number of applied commands (commands[:index] are applied).

docs.title: push(command: Command) -> None
docs.text: Execute command (its redo) and record it. Inside a macro it joins the macro instead of the main stack. Clears the redo branch and may merge into the previous command.

docs.title: set_clean() -> None
docs.text: Mark the current state as saved.

docs.title: Action dataclass
docs.text: A shared trigger for a menu item, a toolbar button, and a shortcut. Keeps one source of truth for text / enabled / checked so the surfaces can't drift apart.

docs.title: trigger() -> bool
docs.text: Fire the action. Toggles checked if checkable. Returns True if it ran (False when disabled).

docs.title: to_menu_item()
docs.text: A components.MenuItem wired to this action.

docs.title: to_tool_button()
docs.text: A shell.ToolButton wired to this action.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Commands and undo

docs.title: elysium.components.completer
docs.text: Autocomplete — a Completer completion popup for text inputs (Qt's QCompleter).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Autocomplete — a completion popup for text inputs (Qt's QCompleter).

A :class:Completer matches a query against a candidate list (prefix first, then optional fuzzy subsequence), exposes keyboard navigation, and paints a popover list anchored under a text field. Wire it to a TextField / EditableComboBox: feed the field's text to :meth:update_query, route Up/Down/Enter/Escape through :meth:on_key, and paint it after the field.

docs.title: Completer dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A completion popup over candidates. x/y/w anchor it (set them to the field's left edge and bottom); the panel grows downward.

docs.title: on_key(key: str) -> bool
docs.text: Route a navigation key. Returns True if consumed.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Completer

docs.title: elysium.components
docs.text: The 30 built-in components, grouped by family.

docs.title: Text (4)
docs.text: Class Purpose Label Static text Heading Larger emphasis text Caption Small de-emphasized text Code Monospace inline text

docs.title: Action (5)
docs.text: Class Purpose Button The primary action IconButton Square icon-only OrbButton Spherical button (Stylized Music Player demo) Link Underlined text action Chip Compact pill action

docs.title: Input (6)
docs.text: Class Purpose TextInput Single-line TextArea Multi-line Slider Numeric range Toggle Boolean switch Checkbox Boolean + tri-state Radio One-of-N picker

docs.title: Container (5)
docs.text: Class Purpose Card Elevated surface; glass material aware Panel Section divider with header Group Visual grouping Divider Hairline rule Accordion Collapsible section list

docs.title: Feedback (4)
docs.text: Class Purpose ProgressBar Linear or radial fill Toast Brief overlay message Spinner Indeterminate loading Tooltip Hover annotation

docs.title: Navigation (3)
docs.text: Class Purpose Tabs Horizontal tab strip Breadcrumb Stacked path Pagination Page selector

docs.title: Overlay (3)
docs.text: Class Purpose Popover Floating panel anchored to a placement Menu Right-click + dropdown menus CommandPalette Fuzzy-search command launcher

docs.title: Phase 3 — self-host primitives (5)
docs.text:
Shipped to support the Elysium Designer's self-host on the framework. Every app gets them for free.

Class Purpose Tree (+ TreeRow) Virtualised hierarchical list with chevron expand + kind-coloured dot + indented label. Author pre-flattens depth; hit_test_row(x, y) returns (row_id, "chevron"|"label") so dispatch is one call. NumericField TextField-style numeric input with Maya-style scrub-on-drag (scrub_start / scrub_drag / scrub_end). Supports min / max clamp + custom format. FAB Floating Action Button. Circular hit-test (not bbox), drop-shadow lift on hover, three variants (primary / accent / surface). RadialPopover Maya-style marking-menu donut. Items are (id, label) pairs evenly spaced clockwise from 12 o'clock; hit_test_item(x, y) returns the wedge under the cursor. IconButton (+ GlyphAtlas + get_default_atlas) Icon-only button that resolves names through a process-wide atlas (skin loader auto-populates from <bundle>/assets/icons/*.png). Falls back to text rendering when no atlas entry.

Quick wiring:

import elysium as ely
from elysium import components as ui, layout

tree = ui.Tree(
    x=10, y=40, w=240, h=400,
    rows=[
        ui.TreeRow(id="r",  label="Project",  expandable=True, expanded=True),
        ui.TreeRow(id="w",  label="MainWindow", depth=1),
        ui.TreeRow(id="b",  label="butterfly",  depth=2, selected=True),
    ],
)

nf = ui.NumericField(
    label="Opacity", value=1.0,
    min_value=0.0, max_value=1.0, step=0.01,
    on_change=lambda v: print("opacity:", v),
)

fab = ui.FAB(
    x=580, y=540, w=56, h=56, icon="A", variant="primary",
    on_click=lambda: print("Aether"),
)

marking = ui.RadialPopover(
    items=[("cut", "Cut"), ("copy", "Copy"),
           ("paste", "Paste"), ("delete", "Delete")],
    visible=True,
)

ib = ui.IconButton(icon="save", variant="ghost",
                   on_click=lambda: print("save"))

docs.title: skin calc(...) preprocessor
docs.text:
elysium.skin.load_skin() resolves any "calc(<expr>)" string in a numeric position before passing the document to the native loader (spec §6.5). The expression grammar is intentionally tight:

  • decimal + scientific-notation numbers
  • operators + - * /
  • parentheses + whitespace

Anything else — identifiers, %, function calls — raises CalcExpressionError at load time. Use it for arithmetic layout constants:

{
  "root": {
    "type": "scene",
    "size": { "w": "calc(800 - 116 - 320)", "h": 480 }
  }
}

docs.title: Base class
docs.text: Class Purpose Component Subclass to author custom components

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Phase 2.5 component library — premium-quality defaults.

Every component

• reads colours + radii + shadows + motion rates from current_theme() • smoothly interpolates hover / pressed / focused progress (no discrete colour swaps) • paints through layered shadow + gradient + body + highlight passes • renders a focus ring in addition to hover lift on focused components

Wiring:

btn = Button(x=100, y=100, w=120, h=40, label="Play",
             on_click=lambda: ...)

# In the per-frame loop:
state = {"hover": btn.hit_test(*cursor) if cursor else False,
         "pressed": pressed and btn.hit_test(*cursor),
         "focused": focused_widget is btn}
btn.update(dt, state)
btn.paint(dl)
if click_just_fired and btn.hit_test(*cursor):
    btn.fire_click()

docs.title: Component dataclass
docs.text: Subclasses override paint(dl). The host calls update(dt, state) each frame before paint.

docs.title: update(dt: float, state: ComponentState) -> None
docs.text: Tick the smooth state-progress values toward their targets.

docs.title: Ripple dataclass
docs.text: Material-style click ripple. Lives on a Button after a click; the Button ticks age each update and removes the ripple when age > 1.

docs.title: Button dataclass
docs.text: Bases: Component

docs.title: fire_click(click_x: float | None = None, click_y: float | None = None) -> None
docs.text: Trigger the on_click handler AND spawn a ripple. Optional (click_x, click_y) place the ripple at the cursor; defaults to the button's centre.

docs.title: IconCloseButton dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Circular close ❌ — used by the butterfly demo. Inherits the theme's smooth state interpolation.

docs.title: GlyphAtlas
docs.text:
Name → image-file registry for IconButtons. Skins ship custom glyph sets under <bundle>/assets/icons/<name>.png; the skin loader (or any app code) populates the atlas by calling atlas.load_from_directory(path) and the IconButton component looks up its icon name to find the file to draw.

The atlas falls back to text rendering when a glyph name has no entry, so authors can mix file-backed and unicode glyphs freely.

docs.title: load_from_directory(path: Any) -> int
docs.text:
Scan path for .png / .svg / .jpg files and register each one under its filename stem. Returns the number of glyphs registered.

Files in subdirectories are ignored this is a flat namespace by design (matches the spec §6.6 layout assets/icons/<name>.png).

docs.title: register(name: str, path: str) -> None
docs.text: Bind name to path directly, bypassing directory scan. Useful for tests + programmatic icon registration.

docs.title: IconButton dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Generic icon-only button. Resolves icon to a sprite via atlas (default: the process-wide atlas) and draws the image inside the bounding box; falls back to rendering icon as text when no atlas entry exists.

Three variants
  • ghost transparent surface, brightens on hover (default).
  • solid theme-primary fill.
  • outline stroked border, transparent fill.

Hit-test is rectangular (bbox). For circular hit-tests use IconCloseButton / FAB.

docs.title: TextField dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Single-line editable text field.

Embeds an :class:elysium.text.edit.EditableText model and implements the :class:elysium.input.Editable protocol, so registering it with a window's InputRouter makes it fully editable: caret, selection, word navigation, undo/redo, clipboard, validators/masks, and IME — no per-app keystroke plumbing. Reads of .value stay valid (mirrored from the model).

docs.title: TextArea dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Multi-line editable text area. Like :class:TextField it embeds an :class:EditableText (with multiline=True) and implements the Editable protocol, adding hard-newline line layout, vertical caret movement, cross-line selection, and vertical scroll. Lines are split on \n; visual soft-wrap of over-long lines is a later refinement.

docs.title: TreeRow dataclass
docs.text:
One row in a Tree. Authors pre-flatten their hierarchy into the display order they want, marking depth + expandable + expanded on each row. The Tree only knows how to paint + hit-test the flat list; expand / collapse logic lives in the host so reactive trees stay simple (the host re-builds the flat list when state changes).

user_data carries any payload the host wants to round-trip through the callback (e.g. a placement index, an asset path).

docs.title: Tree dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Virtualised hierarchical list. Designed for the Designer's Project Explorer Maya-style row layout with a chevron on expandable rows, an optional kind-dot, and the row label. Only the rows currently visible inside (x, y, w, h) actually paint.

Hosts call

tree.rows = build_flat_rows(...) tree.update(dt, {"hover": ...}) tree.paint(dl) hit = tree.hit_test_row(mx, my) # (row_id, "chevron"|"label")

Spec source: §6.1 (250 LOC + tests).

docs.title: hit_test_row(mx: float, my: float) -> tuple[str, str] | None
docs.text: Return (row_id, hit_kind) where hit_kind is "chevron" for the expand toggle area or "label" for the rest of the row. Returns None when the point is outside the tree or below the visible rows.

docs.title: NumericField dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Numeric input field with scrub-on-drag (Maya-style horizontal drag on the label scrubs the value). API:

nf = NumericField(label="X", value=1.0, step=0.1,
                  min_value=0.0, max_value=10.0,
                  on_change=lambda v: ...)
nf.update(dt, {"hover": hover, "focused": focused})
nf.paint(dl)
if drag_started and nf.hit_test(*cursor):
    nf.scrub_start(*cursor)
if dragging: nf.scrub_drag(*cursor)
if drag_released: nf.scrub_end()

The text representation is built by format.format(value). If that fails (custom format strings + edge values) the raw repr is used.

Spec source: §6.3 (80 LOC + tests).

docs.title: FAB dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Floating Action Button. Circular, drop-shadowed, primary-tinted trigger meant for the canonical "one big action" placement (bottom-right corner). Designed for the Aether trigger in the Elysium Designer the Designer wires on_click to a flyout toggle (§12 answer 5).

Hit-test is circular, not bbox.

Spec source: §6.4 (60 LOC + tests).

docs.title: RadialPopover dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Radial marking menu Maya-style donut of wedges around a centre point. Author the items as (id, label) pairs; the component lays them out at evenly-spaced angles starting at the top (12 o'clock) and going clockwise. Set visible=True to animate it in.

Spec source: §6.2 (100 LOC + tests).

docs.title: get_default_atlas() -> GlyphAtlas
docs.text: Process-wide glyph atlas. Use register / load_from_directory to populate it; IconButton consults it when IconButton.icon doesn't resolve via the per-instance atlas override.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Components overview
  • Recipes: custom component

docs.title: elysium.concurrency
docs.text: UiDispatcher, call_on_ui_thread, post, @ui_thread, run_async, and FrameLoop.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Threading → UI marshalling — Tier-2 Qt parity (QMetaObject::invokeMethod / QThread, signals-across-threads).

Elysium's UI runs on one thread (the frame-loop thread that polls input, updates, paints, and publishes the display list). Touching widget state from a worker thread is a data race. This module marshals work back onto the UI thread:

  • :class:UiDispatcher — a thread-safe queue drained once per frame on the UI thread. post(fn) fire-and-forget; invoke(fn) -> Future for a result.
  • :func:call_on_ui_thread / :func:post — module-level convenience over a default dispatcher (also reachable as window.invoke / window.post).
  • :func:ui_thread — decorator that re-dispatches a call onto the UI thread when invoked from a worker (returns a Future); runs inline if already on the UI thread.
  • :class:AsyncRunner / :func:run_async — run asyncio coroutines on a background loop and marshal their results back to the UI thread.
  • :class:FrameLoop — standardizes the per-frame drain so apps stop hand-rolling daemon threads: drain dispatcher → on_frame(dt).

docs.title: UiDispatcher
docs.text: A thread-safe queue of callables run on the UI thread. Workers call :meth:post / :meth:invoke; the frame loop calls :meth:drain each tick (which also marks the calling thread as the UI thread).

docs.title: set_wake(fn: Optional[Callable[[], None]]) -> None
docs.text: Optional callback invoked after enqueue to wake an idle UI loop (with dirty-rect, an idle UI produces no frames).

docs.title: post(fn: Callable, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None
docs.text: Queue fn(*args, **kwargs) to run on the next UI tick.

docs.title: invoke(fn: Callable, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Future
docs.text: Queue fn and return a :class:~concurrent.futures.Future resolved (or excepted) when it runs on the UI thread.

docs.title: drain(max_items: Optional[int] = None) -> int
docs.text: Run queued callables on the current (UI) thread, in order. Returns how many ran. Re-entrant-safe: only items present at entry are run when max_items is None (new posts wait for next tick).

docs.title: AsyncRunner
docs.text: Owns a background thread running an asyncio event loop. Submit coroutines from anywhere; marshal results back to the UI thread.

docs.title: submit(coro) -> Future
docs.text: Schedule coro on the background loop; returns a concurrent.futures.Future.

docs.title: then(coro, on_result: Callable[[Any], None], on_error: Optional[Callable[[BaseException], None]] = None, dispatcher: Optional[UiDispatcher] = None) -> Future
docs.text: Run coro; deliver its result (or error) to on_result / on_error on the UI thread via dispatcher.

docs.title: FrameLoop
docs.text: Standardizes the per-frame cycle so apps stop hand-rolling daemon threads: each tick drains the dispatcher (applying any posted work) then calls on_frame(dt). Run synchronously (drive :meth:tick yourself) or via :meth:start (spawns a daemon thread).

docs.title: call_on_ui_thread(fn: Callable, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Future
docs.text: Marshal fn onto the UI thread; returns a Future for the result.

docs.title: post(fn: Callable, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None
docs.text: Fire-and-forget marshal onto the UI thread.

docs.title: ui_thread(fn: Callable) -> Callable
docs.text: Decorator: calls run inline when already on the UI thread, else are re-dispatched onto it (returning a Future). Use for handlers that touch widget state but may be called from workers.

docs.title: run_async(coro) -> Future
docs.text: Run an asyncio coroutine on the shared background loop. Returns a concurrent.futures.Future (.result(timeout=...) blocks).

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Threading And Services

docs.title: elysium.components.dashboard
docs.text: Dashboard widgets — MetricCard KPI tiles (eyebrow + big value + delta + sparkline) and a persistent Alert/NotificationInbox panel.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Dashboard widgets — KPI metric cards and a persistent alert inbox.

These compose the existing card/badge primitives into the building blocks a business dashboard needs: a :class:MetricCard (eyebrow + big value + delta badge + optional sparkline) and an :class:Alert / :class:NotificationInbox for a persistent "needs attention" panel (distinct from the transient Toast / Snackbar).

docs.title: MetricCard dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A KPI tile: a small label, a big value (caller-formatted), an optional delta badge with a direction, and an optional sub note or inline spark sparkline.

delta_dir is +1 (up) / -1 (down) / 0; good_up says whether up is good — so "Net profit ▲" is green but "Refund drag ▲" is red.

docs.title: Alert dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A persistent, dismissible alert row with a severity tint and an optional action link.

docs.title: NotificationInbox dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A persistent, scrollable list of :class:Alerts — the dashboard's "needs attention" panel.

docs.title: elysium.components.dataentry
docs.text: SpinBox, DoubleSpinBox, DateEdit, TimeEdit, CalendarWidget, and EditableComboBox.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Data-entry widgets — Tier-1 Qt parity.

Spin boxes, date/time editors, a calendar, and an editable combo box — all built on the Phase-2 text-editing core (elysium.text.edit.EditableText) and the Editable protocol, so registering them with a window's InputRouter makes them keyboard-editable with no per-app plumbing.

  • :class:SpinBox / :class:DoubleSpinBox — QSpinBox / QDoubleSpinBox parity: type a number, Up/Down or scroll to step, clamp to range, optional wrap.
  • :class:DateEdit / :class:TimeEdit — segmented editors (Y/M/D, H/M/S): Left/Right select a segment, Up/Down step it, digits type into it.
  • :class:CalendarWidget — month grid; arrows move the day, PageUp/Down the month; click or Enter selects.
  • :class:EditableComboBox — a text field + drop-down list with filter-as-you-type and keyboard selection.

docs.title: SpinBox dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Integer spin box. Type a value, Up/Down (or scroll) to step, clamp to [minimum, maximum], optional wrap. Implements the Editable protocol.

docs.title: DoubleSpinBox dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: SpinBox

Floating-point spin box. Same as SpinBox but value/range are floats and decimals controls precision + the validator.

docs.title: EditableComboBox dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Text field + drop-down list with filter-as-you-type. The text is editable; the popup shows items matching the current text; Up/Down + Enter select, click selects.

docs.text: • Forms And Validation

docs.title: elysium.components.daterange
docs.text: The dashboard date bar — a SegmentedControl and a preset-plus-custom DateRangePicker (preset_range, PRESETS).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Segmented control + date-range picker — the dashboard date bar.

:class:SegmentedControl is the pill-style toggle (Yesterday / 7 days / 30 days / Custom); :class:DateRangePicker wraps it with preset → (start, end) date math and a custom range, matching the date bar both dashboards use.

docs.title: DateRangePicker dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A preset date-range bar. current_range(today) returns the active (start, end); "Custom" uses start / end (set them from your own calendar UI). on_change fires (start, end) on preset change.

docs.title: preset_range(name: str, today: _dt.date) -> tuple[_dt.date, _dt.date] | None
docs.text: (start, end) for a preset name, or None for "Custom".

docs.title: elysium.dialogs
docs.text: Native file pickers plus Elysium-rendered modal dialogs (MessageDialog, InputDialog, ProgressDialog, ColorDialog, FontDialog) managed by DialogHost.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Standard dialogs — Tier-1 Qt parity.

Two families:

  • Native file dialogs (:func:open_file, :func:save_file, :func:pick_folder) — thin wrappers over the rfd-backed native bindings, so users get the real OS picker. Blocking; call from the UI thread.

  • Elysium-rendered modal dialogs (:class:MessageDialog, :class:InputDialog, :class:ProgressDialog, :class:ColorDialog, :class:FontDialog) — borderless, themed, GPU-rendered overlays that match the app's look (the differentiator vs Qt's native chrome). They're driven by a :class:DialogHost the app installs once and ticks/paints each frame. Non-blocking: each dialog resolves via an on_result callback and a pollable .result / .done.

Ergonomic helpers on :class:DialogHost (host.message(...), host.input(...), host.progress(...), host.color(...), host.font(...)) push a dialog and return it.

docs.title: BaseDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Common modal chrome: full-window scrim + centered card + title, an entrance tween, and result plumbing. Subclasses lay out their body and define how a result is produced.

docs.title: dismissed: bool property
docs.text: True once the close animation has fully played out (safe to drop).

docs.title: resolve(value: Any) -> None
docs.text: Settle the dialog with value and begin the close animation.

docs.title: on_mouse_press(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Return True if the press hit a dialog control (so the host stops propagation). Subclasses override + call super for button hits.

docs.title: MessageDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: BaseDialog

Title + body + a row of buttons. Resolves to the clicked button label (Qt QMessageBox parity). Enter accepts the primary button, Esc the last (typically Cancel).

docs.title: InputDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: BaseDialog

Title + prompt + a text field + OK/Cancel. Resolves to the entered string (OK / Enter) or None (Cancel / Esc).

docs.title: ProgressDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: BaseDialog

Title + label + progress bar. Indeterminate by default; call :meth:set_progress with 0..1 for determinate, :meth:close to finish.

docs.title: ColorDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: BaseDialog

Palette grid + alpha slider + hex field. Resolves to an (r,g,b,a) tuple (OK) or None (Cancel). The differentiator: an optional GPU swatch preview. Inspired by the Designer's in-app color picker, decoupled into a reusable component.

docs.title: FontDialog dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: BaseDialog

Family list + size stepper + live preview. Resolves to (family, size) or None.

docs.title: DialogHost
docs.text: Owns the active modal-dialog stack for a window. Install once; call :meth:update + :meth:paint each frame and feed mouse/keys via :meth:on_mouse_press / :meth:on_key. Only the topmost dialog receives input (true modality).

docs.title: open_file(*, title: str | None = None, initial_dir: str | None = None, filter_label: str | None = None, filter_patterns: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> str | None
docs.text: Native open-file dialog. Returns the chosen path or None on cancel.

docs.title: save_file(*, title: str | None = None, initial_dir: str | None = None, default_name: str | None = None, filter_label: str | None = None, filter_patterns: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> str | None
docs.text: Native save-file dialog. Returns the chosen path or None.

docs.title: pick_folder(*, title: str | None = None, initial_dir: str | None = None) -> str | None
docs.text: Native folder-picker dialog. Returns the chosen directory or None.

docs.title: elysium.dnd
docs.text: Inter-widget drag-and-drop — MimeData, DropZone and DragController (Qt's QDrag/QMimeData + drop events).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Inter-widget drag-and-drop — Qt's QDrag / QMimeData / drop events.

A :class:DragController orchestrates a drag: the source arms a drag on press, a small move threshold promotes it to an active drag carrying typed :class:MimeData, registered :class:DropZone\ s highlight as the cursor passes over ones that accept the payload, and releasing over an accepting zone delivers the drop. A drag ghost follows the cursor.

This is in-app, widget-to-widget drag-and-drop (distinct from the native file-drop / outbound-drag in elysium.native).

docs.title: MimeData dataclass
docs.text: A typed payload carried by a drag (Qt's QMimeData). Map MIME-type strings to arbitrary Python values; TEXT is the convenience text slot.

docs.title: DropZone dataclass
docs.text: A region that can accept drops. Give it a rect (or a custom hit), an accept(mime) -> bool predicate, and an on_drop(mime, x, y) -> bool handler.

docs.title: DragController dataclass
docs.text: Routes pointer events into a drag-and-drop interaction over a set of :class:DropZone\ s. The host calls :meth:press / :meth:move / :meth:release with screen coords and :meth:paint each frame.

docs.title: press(mx: float, my: float, mime: MimeData, ghost: Callable[[Any, float, float], None] | None = None, hotspot: tuple[float, float] = (0.0, 0.0), source: Any = None) -> None
docs.text: Arm a potential drag at a press. It becomes active only once the pointer moves past :attr:threshold (so a plain click is unaffected).

docs.title: move(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Returns True once a drag is active. Promotes a pending drag past the threshold and tracks the hovered drop zone.

docs.title: release(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Finish: drop on the accepting zone under the cursor (if any). Returns True if a drop was delivered.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Drag and drop

docs.title: elysium
docs.text: The top-level package. Re-exports the most-used classes from the native extension.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Class Purpose App The application object. One per process. Window An OS window with optional borderless / shaped properties. HookProxy Python-side dotted accessor for skin placements. Canvas A GPU surface inside a placement. Path Vector path builder (move_to / line_to / curve_to / etc.). DisplayList Immutable list of draw commands. SkiaLayer A placement kind owning its own internal DisplayList. Skin A loaded .esk bundle. Image Raster image, loaded from disk or bytes. IpcServer Local IPC server used for hot reload + Code Link.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Function Purpose load_skin(path, surface_size=None) Load a .esk folder into a window. current_app() Return the currently-running App.

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text: Exception When ElysiumError Base of every framework exception. SkinError Skin manifest / document errors. HookNotFound A @window.on(...) named a hook that does not exist. ShaderValidationError A WGSL shader failed validation. CanvasExpired DisplayList submitted to a Canvas that has been replaced.

docs.text: __version__: package version string.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text: Elysium UI — GPU-accelerated, freeform Python UI framework.

docs.title: App
docs.text: Application object. Wraps the native App and returns Python-side Window proxies that support the decorator-based event API.

docs.title: elysium.events

docs.title: Event classes
docs.text: Class Fired by ClickEvent Press + release inside the same placement PressEvent / ReleaseEvent Pointer-down / -up HoverEvent Pointer enter / leave DragEvent Pointer drag (delta_x, delta_y, velocity) FocusEvent / BlurEvent Focus ring movement KeyEvent Keyboard (code, text, modifiers) ChangeEvent Slider / Toggle / TextInput value change WindowEvent Window-level events (resized, focus.gained, etc.)

docs.title: Common fields
docs.text:
event.x, event.y                 # canvas x / y
event.local_x, event.local_y     # local to the placement
event.button                     # 1=primary, 2=secondary, 3=middle
event.modifiers                  # 1=Shift, 2=Ctrl, 4=Alt, 8=Meta
event.target_id                  # which placement was hit
event.stop_propagation()
event.prevent_default()

docs.title: elysium.focus
docs.text: Directional focus navigation. Tab / Shift+Tab cycles by sequence; arrow keys move spatially.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Class Purpose FocusNode(id, bounds, tab_index=None, skip=False, on_focus=None, on_blur=None) One focusable region

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Function Purpose next_focus(nodes, current_id, direction) Resolve the next node given a direction ('next', 'prev', 'up', 'down', 'left', 'right')

docs.title: Per-window helpers
docs.text:
(on Window: re-listed here)

Method Purpose window.install_focus_nav(nodes_provider) Activate focus on this window window.handle_focus_key(code, mods) Pass a key event for focus to consume window.focus_node(id) Programmatic focus window.focused_node Currently focused id or None window.on_focus_changed(fn) Subscribe to focus moves window.set_focus_trap(True) Cycle focus inside this window only

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Path-aware focus navigation.

Tab order follows document order by default. Arrow keys move spatially to the nearest neighbour in the requested direction — "nearest" is the candidate whose centre minimises a weighted distance favouring the axis of motion (90% perpendicular, 10% parallel).

Skins can override per-hook with::

"accessible": {
    "directional_focus": {
        "up": "track.prev", "down": "track.next",
        "left": "album",    "right": "track.title"
    }
}

docs.title: next_focus(nodes: list[FocusNode], current: str | None, direction: Direction) -> str | None
docs.text: Return the node id that should receive focus, or None when no move applies. current may be None to seed focus on the first node in document order.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Focus
  • Events

docs.title: elysium.graphics
docs.text: Interactive 2D canvas — an item scene graph (Scene, Item and built-ins, GraphicsView, SceneController), the equivalent of Qt's QGraphicsScene/QGraphicsView.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Interactive 2D canvas — an item scene graph (Qt's QGraphicsScene / QGraphicsView / QGraphicsItem).

A :class:Scene owns a z-ordered list of :class:Item\ s, each with scene-space bounds, a hit-test, and a paint(dl). Built-in items cover the common shapes (rect / ellipse / line / path / text); subclass :class:Item for anything custom. A :class:GraphicsView (Phase 2) pans/zooms and renders the scene; interaction (select / rubber-band / drag, Phase 3) layers on top.

This module (Tier 5 Phase 1): the scene-graph model + built-in items. Items paint in scene coordinates — the view applies the viewport transform around them — so an item's paint never needs to know the pan/zoom.

docs.title: Item dataclass
docs.text: Base scene item. x/y/w/h are the axis-aligned bounds in scene space. Subclasses override :meth:paint (and usually :meth:contains for a shape-accurate hit-test).

docs.title: contains(sx: float, sy: float) -> bool
docs.text: Hit-test a point in scene space (default: bounds rectangle).

docs.title: LineItem dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Item

A line between two scene points. x/y/w/h are kept in sync as the bounding box so selection + culling work uniformly.

docs.title: PathItem dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Item

An arbitrary SVG-path item. d is in scene coordinates; supply x/y/w/h as its bounding box (used for selection, culling, and the default bounds hit-test).

docs.title: Scene dataclass
docs.text: A z-ordered collection of :class:Item\ s. Painting walks items back to front; hit-testing walks front to back (topmost first).

docs.title: z_sorted() -> list[Item]
docs.text: Items back-to-front (stable within equal z).

docs.title: items_at(sx: float, sy: float) -> list[Item]
docs.text: Visible items containing the scene point, topmost first.

docs.title: items_in_rect(rx: float, ry: float, rw: float, rh: float, contained: bool = False) -> list[Item]
docs.text: Items intersecting (or, if contained, fully inside) a scene rect — the rubber-band query.

docs.title: bounding_rect() -> tuple[float, float, float, float]
docs.text: Union of all item bounds ((0, 0, 0, 0) when empty).

docs.title: paint(dl: Any) -> None
docs.text: Paint every visible item back-to-front, with selection outlines on top. Called by the view inside the viewport transform.

docs.title: GraphicsView dataclass
docs.text:
A rectangular viewport (x/y/w/h in screen space) that pans and zooms over a :class:Scene and renders it (with off-screen culling).

Coordinate model: a scene point maps to the screen as screen = view_origin + (scene - pan) * zoom, where (pan_x, pan_y) is the scene coordinate shown at the view's top-left. :meth:to_view / :meth:to_scene convert between the spaces; pointer handlers should map screen coords to scene coords with :meth:to_scene before querying the scene.

docs.title: zoom_at(vx: float, vy: float, factor: float) -> None
docs.text: Zoom by factor while keeping the scene point under (vx, vy) fixed on screen (cursor-anchored zoom).

docs.title: pan_by(dvx: float, dvy: float) -> None
docs.text: Pan by a screen-pixel delta.

docs.title: fit(margin: float = 24.0, rect: tuple[float, float, float, float] | None = None) -> None
docs.text: Pan + zoom so rect (or the whole scene) fills the viewport with a margin (screen px) border.

docs.title: visible_items() -> list[Item]
docs.text: Scene items (back-to-front) that intersect the viewport — i.e. what :meth:paint actually draws after culling.

docs.title: SceneController dataclass
docs.text:
The interaction layer over a :class:GraphicsView: click-select (with additive multi-select), rubber-band selection, drag-to-move, and resize handles for a single resizable selection. Optional grid snap.

The host feeds it screen-space pointer coords; it maps to the scene via the view. Call :meth:paint_overlay after the view paints, so the rubber-band rect and handles draw on top in screen space.

docs.title: handle_rects() -> dict[str, tuple[float, float, float, float]]
docs.text: Screen-space handle squares for a single resizable selection (empty otherwise). Handles are constant-size regardless of zoom.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Interactive 2D canvas

docs.title: elysium.modelview.grid
docs.text: DataGrid — an Excel-grade editable spreadsheet over ItemModel: frozen columns, range select, copy/paste TSV, fill-down, per-cell validation badges and pending-edit highlighting.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
DataGrid — an Excel-grade editable spreadsheet over :class:ItemModel.

Builds on the existing Model/View (ItemModel + Column) and adds the spreadsheet features a bulk editor needs that the virtualized TableView doesn't: frozen/pinned leading columns, column resize / reorder / show-hide, rectangular range selection, copy / paste TSV (paste an Excel block into a cell range), fill-down, per-cell validation badges, and per-cell pending-edit highlighting. Rows are virtualized (only the visible window paints), so a 100k-row catalog stays at frame rate.

Column sorting (sortable, on by default) and a per-column filter row (filterable, off by default) are both optional and configurable: sorting honours each :class:Column's sortable flag and cycles asc → desc → unsorted on a header click; filtering honours Column.filterable, shows a search box under each header, and narrows the body live. Both delegate to the underlying :class:ItemModel (toggle_sort / filter), so cell state and virtualization keep working.

Cell state (dirty / error) is keyed by the row object identity, so it stays correct across sorts and filters. copy() / paste(text) operate on plain TSV strings (so they're testable and clipboard-agnostic); copy_to_clipboard() is the best-effort system-clipboard convenience.

docs.title: DataGrid dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A spreadsheet grid bound to an :class:ItemModel. Selection, copy/paste, fill-down, frozen columns, and per-cell validation/pending state, with virtualized row painting.

docs.title: cell_at(mx: float, my: float) -> Optional[tuple]
docs.text: (view_row, visible_col) for a screen point in the body, else None.

docs.title: header_col_at(mx: float, my: float) -> Optional[int]
docs.text: Visible-column index whose header label is under the point, or None.

docs.title: filter_cell_at(mx: float, my: float) -> Optional[str]
docs.text: Column key of the filter box under the point, or None.

docs.title: header_border_at(mx: float, my: float, grab: float = 4.0)
docs.text: The column key whose right header border is under mx (for a resize), or None.

docs.title: cursor_at(mx: float, my: float, grab: float = 4.0) -> Optional[str]
docs.text:
The mouse-cursor the grid wants at (mx, my): "ew-resize" — the horizontal double-arrow (↔) resize affordance — while a column resize is in progress, or when hovering the draggable border between two column headers; otherwise None.

This tells the user where to click-and-drag to resize a column. It is a hint; the app applies it each frame against the live cursor position. Coordinates are window-local (the same space as win.cursor_position and the grid's own x/y), so pass the cursor straight through::

pos = win.cursor_position
if pos is not None:
    win.set_cursor(grid.cursor_at(pos[0], pos[1]) or "default")

(grab widens the hover hotspot on each side of the exact border.)

docs.title: selected_range() -> Optional[tuple]
docs.text: (r0, c0, r1, c1) normalized, or None.

docs.title: sort_by(key: str) -> None
docs.text: Cycle column key asc → desc → unsorted. No-op when sorting is disabled (self.sortable) or the column is not Column.sortable.

docs.title: active_filters() -> dict
docs.text: The live {col_key: query} map (non-empty queries only).

docs.title: set_filter(key: str, query: str) -> None
docs.text: Set (or clear, if query is empty) the search text for column key and re-apply. No-op when the column is not Column.filterable.

docs.title: on_text(text: str) -> None
docs.text: Append typed text to the focused filter box (no-op if none focused).

docs.title: on_backspace() -> None
docs.text: Delete the last character of the focused filter box.

docs.title: copy() -> str
docs.text: The selected range as TSV (rows by \n, cells by \t).

docs.title: paste(text: str) -> int
docs.text: Paste TSV starting at the active (or anchor) cell. Returns the number of cells written. New values are marked dirty + validated.

docs.title: fill_down() -> int
docs.text: Copy the top row of the selection down to the rest. Returns cells written.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• The data grid
  • elysium.modelview

docs.title: elysium.i18n
docs.text: Translation (tr, tr_n), locale + per-string direction detection, and RTL layout-mirroring helpers.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Internationalization — Tier-2 Qt parity (QTranslator / tr(), RTL layout).

Translation is gettext-first (Python's stdlib gettext + compiled .mo catalogs — the standard translator toolchain), with a JSON-catalog fast path for simple apps. Plus right-to-left support: locale + per-string direction detection (Arabic/Hebrew/…) and horizontal layout-mirroring helpers.

from elysium.i18n import tr, tr_n, install
install("fr", localedir="locale")      # loads locale/fr/LC_MESSAGES/<domain>.mo
label = tr("Save")                     # → "Enregistrer"
msg = tr_n("{n} file", "{n} files", n) # plural-aware

Mark translatable strings with tr("...") so xgettext/pybabel can extract them. Locale-aware number/date formatting lives in :mod:elysium.locale.

docs.title: install(locale: str, *, localedir: Optional[str] = None, domain: str = 'messages') -> None
docs.text: Load and activate the gettext catalog for locale from localedir (<localedir>/<locale>/LC_MESSAGES/<domain>.mo). Falls back to an identity translator (returns msgids) when no catalog is found.

docs.title: set_locale(locale: str) -> None
docs.text: Switch locale, re-resolving the active gettext domain. Re-render the UI on the next frame to apply the new strings.

docs.title: use_translation(translation: _gettext.NullTranslations, locale: str = 'en') -> None
docs.text: Install an already-constructed gettext translation (e.g. from a test fixture or a custom loader).

docs.title: load_json_catalog(mapping: dict[str, str], locale: str = 'en') -> None
docs.text: Install a translator from a plain {msgid: msgstr} dict — a quick path for apps that don't want the gettext toolchain.

docs.title: tr(message: str, **kwargs: Any) -> str
docs.text: Translate message; if kwargs are given, str.format the result with them (tr("Hi {name}", name=x)).

docs.title: tr_n(singular: str, plural: str, n: int, **kwargs: Any) -> str
docs.text: Plural-aware translation. n selects the form; it's also passed to str.format as n along with any kwargs.

docs.title: tr_ctx(context: str, message: str, **kwargs: Any) -> str
docs.text: Context-qualified translation (gettext pgettext).

docs.title: text_is_rtl(s: str) -> bool
docs.text: True when the first strong-directional character is right-to-left (the Unicode first-strong heuristic).

docs.title: current_direction() -> str
docs.text: Active layout direction: an explicit :func:direction override, else derived from the current locale.

docs.title: direction(d: str) -> Iterator[None]
docs.text: Temporarily force layout direction (LTR / RTL) regardless of locale — for previews and mixed-direction sub-trees.

docs.title: mirror_x(x: float, item_w: float, container_w: float) -> float
docs.text: Reflect an x-position within container_w (left edge ↔ right edge). Use when laying out under RTL so left-anchored items become right-anchored.

docs.title: maybe_mirror_x(x: float, item_w: float, container_w: float) -> float
docs.text: :func:mirror_x when the active direction is RTL, else x unchanged.

docs.title: flip_align(align: str) -> str
docs.text: Swap left/right for RTL; center/justify pass through.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Threading And Services

docs.title: elysium.input
docs.text: Per-window keyboard / text / IME / clipboard dispatch to the focused widget, plus focus navigation.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Framework-level input routing + focus management.

Until now every Elysium app hand-rolled its own poll_key_event loop and mutated widget state directly (see examples/*/main.py). Editable widgets (text fields, spin boxes, editable table cells) need a central router that delivers keystrokes, typed text, IME composition, and clipboard actions to whichever widget currently holds focus — the retained-interaction layer Qt gets from QApplication event dispatch + QWidget.setFocus.

This module provides three pieces:

  • :class:Focusable / :class:Editable — duck-typed protocols a widget implements to participate. A widget is focusable if it has a stable focus_id and a focus_rect; editable if it additionally accepts keys / text / IME.
  • :class:FocusManager — owns the current focused id and moves focus via Tab / Shift-Tab / arrows, reusing :func:elysium.focus.next_focus.
  • :class:InputRouter — bound to a window; each frame it drains the native key queue + IME preedit and routes them to the focused editable, handles focus-navigation keys the widget didn't consume, orchestrates cut/copy/paste against the system clipboard, and keeps the OS IME candidate popup parked at the caret.

The router never owns rendering — widgets still paint themselves each frame. It owns interaction: turning raw OS input into method calls on the focused widget.

docs.title: Focusable
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

A widget that can hold keyboard focus.

docs.title: focus_rect() -> tuple[float, float, float, float]
docs.text: Bounding rect (x, y, w, h) in window coords, used for spatial (arrow-key) focus navigation and the focus ring.

docs.title: Editable
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

A focusable widget that consumes text input.

All methods are optional in practice — the router guards each call — but a real text widget implements at least on_key + on_text.

docs.title: wants_keys() -> bool
docs.text: Return True while this widget should receive key/text input (e.g. it's enabled and in an editable mode).

docs.title: on_key(code: str, mods: int) -> bool
docs.text: Handle a control key (arrows, Backspace, Home, Ctrl+A, …). Return True if consumed; False lets the router try focus navigation (so e.g. an unhandled Tab still moves focus).

docs.title: on_text(text: str) -> None
docs.text: Insert typed text at the caret (a single grapheme, usually).

docs.title: on_ime_preedit(text: str) -> None
docs.text: Update the in-flight IME composition string (rendered with an underline); empty string clears it.

docs.title: on_ime_commit(text: str) -> None
docs.text: Commit a finished IME composition as inserted text.

docs.title: caret_rect() -> Optional[tuple[float, float, float, float]]
docs.text: Caret rect (x, y, w, h) in window coords for IME popup placement, or None if unavailable.

docs.title: FocusManager
docs.text:
Owns the focused-widget id and moves focus between focusables.

Focus navigation reuses :func:elysium.focus.next_focus (document-order Tab, spatial arrows) over the live focusable rects.

docs.title: on_change(fn) -> None
docs.text: Register fn(new_id | None) fired whenever focus moves.

docs.title: move(direction: str, widgets: list[Any]) -> str | None
docs.text: Move focus in direction ("next"/"prev"/"up"/"down"/ "left"/"right"). Returns the new focused id (or None).

docs.title: InputRouter
docs.text: Per-window input dispatcher. Call :meth:set_widgets each frame (or whenever the focusable set changes) and :meth:tick once per frame after the window has polled — it drains the native key queue + IME preedit and routes everything to the focused widget.

docs.title: set_widgets(widgets: Iterable[Any]) -> None
docs.text: Replace the focusable widget set. Widgets must expose focus_id + focus_rect; editables additionally implement the :class:Editable methods. Safe to call every frame.

docs.title: set_scrollables(scrollables: Iterable[Any]) -> None
docs.text: Register the scrollable widgets (scroll_rect + on_scroll). Mouse-wheel deltas are routed to whichever one is under the cursor. Listed back-to-front; the last (topmost) match wins. Safe per frame.

docs.title: tick() -> None
docs.text: Drain input and route it. Call once per frame.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Text Input And Dialogs
  • Forms And Validation

docs.title: elysium.layout
docs.text: The five layout containers plus alignment constants.

docs.title: Containers
docs.text: Class Axis Children Stack Z (overlay) Anything Row X (horizontal) Anything Col Y (vertical) Anything Grid XY Anything; Grid.Cell for span control Form Y (label + control pairs) Form.Row(label, control)

docs.title: Helpers
docs.text: Class / fn Purpose Spacer Consumes remaining space in a Row / Col Grid.Cell(child, row=, column=, row_span=, column_span=) Explicit grid placement Form.Row(label, control) Labeled form row

docs.title: Alignment constants
docs.text:
START, CENTER, END, STRETCH, BETWEEN, AROUND, EVENLY.

Both string ("center") and constant (CENTER) forms are accepted.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Layout primitives. The single biggest API gap relative to Qt.

Stack(direction, spacing, padding, align, justify) is the workhorse — all other layouts (Row, Col, Grid, Form) compose from it.

from elysium import layout
root = layout.Col(spacing=12, padding=16, children=[
    ui.Label(text="Account"),
    layout.Row(spacing=8, children=[
        ui.TextField(w=240),
        ui.Button(label="Save"),
    ]),
])
root.layout(x=0, y=0, w=app_w, h=app_h)
root.paint(dl)

Components stay absolute-positioned internally; the layout containers reposition them by mutating x/y/w/h during layout(). That keeps the runtime painter simple (no second pass) while letting authors describe intent declaratively.

docs.title: Stack dataclass
docs.text:
A flex-box-ish container.

direction : 'row' | 'col' spacing : pixels between children padding : (top, right, bottom, left) or scalar align : 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'stretch' — cross-axis justify : 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'between' | 'around' | 'evenly' — main-axis distribution grow : per-child flex weights, list[float] (defaults to all-zero)

docs.title: Grid dataclass
docs.text: Uniform grid: cols columns, evenly-spaced cells. Children flow left-to-right, top-to-bottom.

docs.title: Form(spacing: float = 8.0, label_w: float = 120.0, padding: float | tuple = 0.0, rows: Iterable[tuple[Any, Any]] = ()) -> Stack
docs.text: Label / field rows. Each row is a Row with a fixed-width label.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Layout
  • Components overview

docs.title: elysium.locale
docs.text: Locale-aware number, currency, date, and time formatting (Babel-backed when installed).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Locale-aware formatting — Tier-2 Qt parity (QLocale).

Formats numbers, currency, dates, and times for a locale. Uses Babel (full CLDR) when it's installed; otherwise falls back to a small built-in table for common locales — no hard dependency, thread-safe (never touches the global setlocale).

from elysium import locale as L
L.format_number(1234567.5, locale="de")   # "1.234.567,5"
L.format_currency(9.9, "USD", locale="en") # "$9.90"
L.format_date(date.today(), locale="fr")

docs.title: format_number(value: float, locale: Optional[str] = None) -> str
docs.text: Group-separated number in the locale's convention.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Threading And Services

docs.title: elysium.modelview
docs.text: ItemModel (+ QtItemModelAdapter), virtualized TableView/ListView/TreeView, and delegates incl. the GPU Mesh3DDelegate.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Model/View — Tier-1 Qt parity for data-driven lists, tables, and trees.

Qt's Model/View separates what the data is (a model) from how it's shown (a view) with pluggable per-cell renderers (delegates). Elysium's version is idiomatic and reactive — :class:ItemModel holds rows, derives a sorted/filtered view lazily, and bumps a version counter on change so the immediate-mode views repaint — plus a thin :class:QtItemModelAdapter (rowCount/columnCount/data/setData…) so developers porting from QAbstractItemModel feel at home.

Views (:class:TableView, :class:ListView, :class:TreeView) are virtualized — only the rows visible in their rect are painted, so a 100k-row model stays at frame rate. Cells render through a :class:Delegate (:class:TextDelegate, :class:EditableCellDelegate, and the differentiator :class:Mesh3DDelegate for a GPU 3-D thumbnail per cell).

docs.title: ItemModel
docs.text: A list of row records (dicts or objects) + column metadata. Derives a sorted/filtered view lazily and version-stamps every mutation so views know when to rebuild. Pair with reactive signal in app code if you want push updates; the views themselves poll per frame.

docs.title: rows() -> list[Any]
docs.text: The source rows in insertion order (unsorted, unfiltered). Use :meth:view for the sorted/filtered display order.

docs.title: toggle_sort(key: str) -> None
docs.text: Cycle a column: asc → desc → unsorted.

docs.title: QtItemModelAdapter
docs.text: QAbstractItemModel-shaped facade over an :class:ItemModel, for developers porting from PySide6/Qt. Roles mirror Qt's ints (DisplayRole=0, EditRole=2).

docs.title: EditableCellDelegate dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: TextDelegate

Same rendering as TextDelegate, but the view may swap in a TextField editor when the cell is double-clicked / Enter-activated.

docs.title: Mesh3DDelegate dataclass
docs.text: Differentiator: render a small GPU 3-D thumbnail per cell. The cell value is a mesh spec (path or library name); we render it once via the PBR engine and cache the PNG, falling back to a colored chip while it loads / if rendering is unavailable. Lets a data table show real 3-D previews — impossible in Qt's item views without a custom OpenGL widget.

docs.title: TableView dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Virtualized, columnar data table over an :class:ItemModel. Clickable sort headers, horizontal scroll, row selection, and editable cells via an :class:EditableCellDelegate. Only the rows visible in (x,y,w,h) paint.

docs.title: header_border_at(mx: float, my: float, grab: float = 4.0) -> Optional[str]
docs.text: The column key whose right header border is under mx (a resize target), or None. Only within the header band.

docs.title: resize_col(key: str, width: float) -> None
docs.text: Set column key's width, clamped to a sensible minimum (40px).

docs.title: cursor_at(mx: float, my: float, grab: float = 4.0) -> Optional[str]
docs.text: "ew-resize" — the horizontal double-arrow (↔) resize affordance — over a column-header border, or while a resize is in progress; else None. Coordinates are window-local (the same space as win.cursor_position and the view's x/y); apply it each frame, e.g. win.set_cursor(table.cursor_at(*win.cursor_position) or "default").

docs.title: on_mouse_drag(mx: float, my: float) -> None
docs.text: Continue an in-progress column resize; no-op otherwise, so an app can forward every drag here safely. Pairs with :meth:on_mouse_release.

docs.title: on_mouse_release() -> None
docs.text: End a column resize (drop the grabbed header border).

docs.title: ListView dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: TableView

Single-column virtualized list. Convenience over TableView with one column and no header.

docs.title: TreeView dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Virtualized hierarchical view. Rows are supplied pre-flattened with a depth (like the existing Tree), built from a nested model via :func:flatten_tree. Reuses the row-range virtualization pattern.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Tables And Modelview
  • Crud App

docs.title: elysium.native
docs.text: Single-instance, notifications, system tray (Tray), and global hotkeys (HotKeys), capability-gated per platform.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Native OS integration — Tier-2 Qt parity (QSystemTrayIcon, QShortcut / global hotkeys, notifications, single-instance).

A capability-gated wrapper over the native layer. Features that aren't available on a platform degrade gracefully and are reported by :func:capabilities — per the project's GTK-free Linux stance, system tray and global hotkeys are macOS/Windows-only, while notifications work everywhere (native on macOS/Windows, notify-send on Linux) and single-instance is pure-Python-friendly everywhere.

import elysium.native as native
if not native.single_instance("dev.example.app"):
    sys.exit("already running")
native.notify("Build finished", "All tests passed")

tray = native.Tray("MyApp", [("open", "Open"), ("quit", "Quit")])
tray.on("quit", lambda: app.quit())
tray.create()                 # call on the main thread during setup
# each frame: tray.poll()

keys = native.HotKeys()
keys.register(native.CTRL | native.SHIFT, "KeyR", on_reload)
# each frame: keys.poll()

docs.title: Tray
docs.text: A system-tray icon with a context menu. Construct with (id, label) items, wire on(id, handler) callbacks, call :meth:create on the main thread during setup, then :meth:poll each frame to dispatch clicks.

docs.title: poll() -> Optional[str]
docs.text: Dispatch any pending menu click to its handler; returns the id.

docs.title: HotKeys
docs.text: System-wide global hotkeys. register(mods, key, handler) then :meth:poll each frame to dispatch fired hotkeys to their handlers.

docs.title: register(mods: int, key: str, handler: Optional[Callable[[], None]] = None) -> int
docs.text: Register a hotkey; returns its non-zero id (0 = failed/unsupported).

docs.title: poll() -> Optional[int]
docs.text: Dispatch any fired hotkey to its handler; returns the hotkey id.

docs.title: capabilities() -> dict[str, bool]
docs.text: Map of native feature → supported on this platform.

docs.title: single_instance(app_id: str) -> bool
docs.text: Return True if this is the first running instance for app_id (or it already holds the lock); False if another instance is running. If the native lock is unavailable, returns True so the app still starts.

docs.title: notify(title: str, body: str = '', app_name: str = 'Elysium') -> bool
docs.text: Show an OS notification (best-effort). Native on macOS/Windows, notify-send on Linux. Returns True on success.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Threading And Services

docs.title: elysium.pack
docs.text: Cross-platform signed bundle packaging. The Python surface that elysium pack calls into.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Function Purpose pack(entry, name, identifier, icon=None, includes=[], sign=True, notarize=False) Build a per-OS bundle available_signers() Discover signing identities on the local machine verify_bundle(path) Verify signatures on a built bundle

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: OS Output macOS dist/<Name>.app (signed if ELYSIUM_SIGNING_IDENTITY set; notarized if --notarize) Windows dist/<Name>/<Name>.exe + installer .exe Linux dist/<Name>.AppImage (+ .deb if dpkg-deb is on PATH)

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text: Var Effect ELYSIUM_SIGNING_IDENTITY macOS Developer ID ELYSIUM_NOTARY_PROFILE macOS notarytool keychain profile ELYSIUM_WINDOWS_CERT Path to a Windows PFX ELYSIUM_WINDOWS_CERT_PASSWORD Password

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Elysium one-click packager.

elysium pack myapp.py produces a self-contained distributable for the host platform:

  • macOS: MyApp.app/ bundle with embedded Python, signed when an identity is provided, optionally notarized + stapled.
  • Windows: MyApp.exe + site-packages/ + Python DLLs in a single folder; an optional MyApp.msi when WiX is present.
  • Linux: MyApp.AppImage (self-mounting squashfs) when appimagetool is on PATH, otherwise a portable MyApp/ directory with a launch script.

docs.title: Design notes
docs.text:
• The interpreter is fetched from python-build-standalone (https://github.com/astral-sh/python-build-standalone) the first time the user packages — relocatable, reproducible, no system Python dependency.
  • Dependencies are resolved from the active venv's site-packages plus whatever modulefinder reports — the union catches both declared installs and lazy imports.
  • The native cdylib (elysium/_native/_native.{so,pyd}) is copied as a peer.
  • A tiny launcher (Rust binary template, also pure-Python fallback) forks the embedded Python and runs the user's entry script.

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
.. code-block:: python

from elysium.pack import pack
pack(
    entry="app/main.py",
    name="My App",
    version="0.1.0",
    identifier="dev.example.myapp",
    icon="assets/icon.png",
    output_dir="dist/",
)

docs.title: CLI
docs.text: elysium pack <entry> [--name=...] [--version=...] [--icon=...] [--identifier=...] [--output=dist/] [--sign-identity=...] [--notarize] [--no-python]

docs.title: pack(entry: str | Path, **kwargs) -> Path
docs.text: Build a self-contained distributable. Returns the output bundle path.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Packaging
  • CLI

docs.title: elysium.reactive
docs.text: Fine-grained reactive primitives, modeled on Solid.js.

docs.title: Primitives
docs.text: Function Purpose signal(value) Create a mutable reactive cell computed(fn) Lazy derived read effect(fn) Side-effecting callback that re-runs on signal changes

docs.title: Class
docs.text: Class Purpose Signal Type of values returned by signal(...)

docs.title: Helpers
docs.text: Function Purpose batch(fn) Run fn and defer effect re-runs to its end untrack(fn) Read signals without registering dependencies on_cleanup(fn) Register a cleanup inside an effect

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Phase 2.2 reactive layer — fine-grained signal / computed / effect.

Design follows the Solid.js / Vue 3 model: reads inside a tracking scope register dependencies automatically; writes invalidate downstream subscribers and re-run them.

v = signal(1)
doubled = computed(lambda: v() * 2)
@effect
def log_doubled():
    print("doubled =", doubled())   # prints "doubled = 2"
v.set(5)                              # prints "doubled = 10"

docs.title: Signal
docs.text:
Bases: Generic[T]

A reactive cell. Reads inside an effect / computed register a subscription; set() notifies every subscriber.

docs.title: peek() -> T
docs.text: Read without subscribing — useful inside effects that don't want to depend on this signal.

docs.title: computed(fn: Callable[[], T]) -> Callable[[], T]
docs.text: Memoized derived value. Returns a callable; call it to read, and computed values transparently propagate dependencies.

docs.title: effect(fn: Callable[[], None]) -> Callable[[], None]
docs.text: Run fn immediately and re-run whenever any signal it read changes. Returns a dispose callable that unsubscribes the effect.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Reactive
  • Recipes: signal + computed

docs.title: elysium.render
docs.text: PBR rendering, texture pipeline, GPU compute, and preview helpers.

docs.title: Submodules

docs.title: pbr
docs.text: Symbol Purpose Material PBR material MeshObject Mesh + materials bundle import_mesh_from_file(path) Load .3ds / .obj / .gltf / .glb to_environment(studio) Convert a studio dict to an environment load_hdri(path, intensity) Load .hdr / .exr render_mesh(w, h, obj, env, ...) CPU BVH render render_path_traced(w, h, obj, env, samples, ...) Monte Carlo path tracer STUDIOS Dict of 8 light-studio presets PRESETS Dict of ~20 material presets

docs.title: texture
docs.text: Symbol Purpose extract_from_file(path, name) Make a tileable from an image PaintMask(w, h) Per-placement paint surface TextureLayer(path, ...) One layer in a composite composite_layers(layers, w, h) Composite layers to RGBA enhance(path, scale) AI / Lanczos upscaler procedural.perlin_noise, .voronoi, .checker, .linear_gradient Procedural tile generators

docs.title: compute
docs.text: Symbol Purpose dispatch(shader_wgsl, w, h, entry='main') Dispatch a compute shader; return RGBA render_mesh_gpu(w, h, obj, env) GPU PBR preview path

docs.title: preview

docs.text:
• PBR
  • Textures

docs.title: elysium.text.richtext
docs.text: Rich text — styled-run documents (Run/Image/Break → RichDocument) and a RichTextView widget (Qt's QTextDocument + view).

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Rich text — styled-run documents (Qt's QTextDocument + a view widget).

A :class:RichDocument is a sequence of styled :class:Run\ s (bold / italic / size / colour / family / underline / hyperlink) and inline :class:Image\ s, with paragraph :class:Break\ s. :meth:RichDocument.layout word-wraps them to a width into baseline-aligned placements; :class:RichTextView renders a document and hit-tests hyperlinks.

Layout uses the native measure_text_run (advance + ascent/descent) for metrics and renders each run through the Skia paragraph path (draw_paragraph), so bold (weight) and italic (a slant variation axis) are real font styling, not faux effects.

docs.title: Run dataclass
docs.text: A styled span of text.

docs.title: Image dataclass
docs.text: An inline image (a path to decode, or just a sized placeholder).

docs.title: Break
docs.text: A hard paragraph break.

docs.title: Placement dataclass
docs.text: A laid-out run/image fragment in document coordinates (relative to the document origin). baseline is the text baseline y.

docs.title: RichDocument dataclass
docs.text: An ordered list of Run / Image / Break items.

docs.title: layout(width: float) -> tuple[list[Placement], float]
docs.text: Word-wrap to width. Returns (placements, total_height) in document coordinates (origin at 0, 0). Each placement carries its own ascent in _asc so the second (baseline) pass aligns mixed sizes.

docs.title: RichTextView dataclass
docs.text: Renders a :class:RichDocument at x/y wrapped to w, and hit-tests hyperlinks. Set on_link to handle clicks.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Rich text
  • Documents and editing

docs.title: elysium.components.scroll
docs.text: ScrollView (clipped scrollable viewport with momentum) and ScrollBar.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Scroll system — Tier-2 Qt parity (QScrollBar / QScrollArea).

  • :class:ScrollBar — a draggable track + thumb with click-to-page and auto-hide, driven by content/viewport lengths (offset in logical pixels).
  • :class:ScrollView — a clipped, scrollable viewport: it translates + clips its content (via the display list's push_clip / push_transform) and owns both scrollbars, mouse-wheel + drag scrolling, and flick momentum.

Wheel events arrive through the window's poll_scroll_delta and are routed to the hovered scrollable by the :class:elysium.input.InputRouter (see its set_scrollables). Both implement the lightweight Scrollable protocol (scroll_rect + on_scroll).

docs.title: ScrollBar dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A scrollbar over a content range. value is the scroll offset in logical pixels in [0, max_offset]; content / viewport are the total + visible lengths along the bar's axis.

docs.title: thumb_extent() -> tuple[float, float]
docs.text: Return (start, length) of the thumb along the track.

docs.title: ScrollView dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A clipped scrollable viewport over content of size (content_w, content_h). Call :meth:paint with a callback that draws the content in content-space (origin = content top-left). Implements the Scrollable protocol so the InputRouter can deliver wheel events.

docs.title: scroll_to_rect(rx: float, ry: float, rw: float, rh: float) -> None
docs.text: Scroll the minimum amount so the content-space rect is visible.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Scale And Scroll

docs.title: elysium.settings
docs.text: Settings — a QSettings-equivalent per-user store with dotted groups and atomic writes.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Per-user settings — Tier-2 Qt parity (QSettings).

A small, dependency-free persistence layer for app config: typed get/set with dotted groups, durable atomic writes, an in-memory cache, change callbacks, and platform-appropriate storage locations. Generalizes the Designer's hand-rolled ~/.elysium/designer-prefs.json pattern.

s = Settings("designer")            # ~/.elysium/designer/settings.json
s.set("window.size", [1200, 800])
w, h = s.get("window.size", [800, 600])
with s.group("palette"):
    s.set("recent", ["#ff0000"])    # stored under "palette.recent"
s.save()                            # or pass autosave=True

Keys are dotted paths into a nested dict, so window.size and window.pos share the window group.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Persistent per-user key/value store with dotted-key groups.

docs.title: save() -> None
docs.text: Atomically write to disk (temp file + os.replace) so a crash mid-write never corrupts the existing settings.

docs.title: keys() -> list[str]
docs.text: All stored dotted keys (leaves), depth-first.

docs.title: group(name: str) -> Iterator['Settings']
docs.text: Scope keys under name. for the duration of the block. Nestable.

docs.title: on_change(fn: Callable[[str, Any], None]) -> None
docs.text: Register fn(full_key, value) fired on every set that actually changes a value.

docs.title: config_dir(app: str) -> Path
docs.text:
Platform-appropriate per-user config directory for app.

  • Linux/BSD: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/elysium/<app> (default ~/.config)
  • macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/elysium/<app>
  • Windows: %APPDATA%/elysium/<app>

Always rooted under an elysium namespace so apps don't collide.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Threading And Services
  • Crud App

docs.title: elysium.shell
docs.text: Application-shell widgets — the structural frame for desktop apps: GroupBox, StatusBar, Splitter, MenuBar, ToolBar/ToolButton, TabWidget, DockManager/DockWidget, plus the flow widgets Drawer, Stepper and Wizard.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Application-shell widgets — the structural frame Elysium was missing for Qt/PySide6 parity (the QMainWindow ecosystem).

These compose the framework's existing immediate-mode Component primitives (Button, Label, Menu, Tabs …) into the layout widgets a real desktop app needs. Every widget is an immediate-mode @dataclass Component: it reads current_theme() at paint time (so it recolours with the theme) and exposes plain methods the host calls to dispatch input.

Tier 4 — App-shell essentials. This module (Phase 1):

  • :class:GroupBox — a titled, bordered container with a content rect.
  • :class:StatusBar — a bottom bar: a transient message + right-aligned permanent sections.
  • :class:Splitter — a draggable divider splitting an area into two panes (horizontal or vertical), with min-size clamping.
  • :class:MenuBar — a persistent in-window menu bar over the existing Menu / MenuItem popovers.

Later phases add ToolBar/ToolButton, TabWidget, and DockManager/DockWidget.

docs.title: GroupBox dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A titled panel with a header row, a hairline divider, and a content area. Lay child widgets out inside :meth:content_rect.

docs.title: content_rect() -> tuple[float, float, float, float]
docs.text: The inner rect (below the header, inset by pad) for children.

docs.title: StatusBar dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A thin bar (Qt's QStatusBar): a transient left-aligned message and a list of right-aligned permanent sections (e.g. cursor pos, encoding).

docs.title: Splitter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Splits its area into two panes with a draggable handle.

orientation="horizontal" → left | right panes with a vertical handle; "vertical" → top / bottom panes with a horizontal handle. ratio is the first pane's fraction of the total. The caller lays its two children into :meth:pane_rects, paints the splitter, and routes presses/drags via :meth:on_press / :meth:on_drag / :meth:on_release.

docs.title: on_press(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Begin a drag if the press is on (or near) the handle.

docs.title: MenuBar dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A horizontal strip of menu titles; clicking one opens its dropdown Menu. Built on the existing Menu / MenuItem components.

menus is a list of (title, [MenuItem, ...]). The host calls :meth:on_click to toggle a menu, paints the bar, then paints :meth:open_menu (if any) last so the dropdown overlays siblings, and routes dropdown clicks through :meth:dispatch_open_click.

docs.title: title_rects() -> list[tuple[int, str, float, float]]
docs.text: (index, title, x, width) for each title, left-to-right.

docs.title: on_click(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Toggle the clicked title's menu. Returns True if a title was hit.

docs.title: open_menu() -> Menu | None
docs.text: The dropdown Menu for the open title, positioned under it (or None if nothing is open). Paint this last.

docs.title: dispatch_open_click(mx: float, my: float) -> bool
docs.text: Route a click while a dropdown is open: fire the item's callback (or just close). Returns True if the click was consumed by the dropdown layer (so the host should not also treat it as a canvas click).

docs.title: ToolButton dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A compact icon (and/or label) button for toolbars. icon is a painter (dl, cx, cy, size, color) -> None so any glyph source can be plugged in (a lambda, a framework GlyphAtlas, or the Designer's icon registry). checked renders the toggled/active state.

docs.title: ToolBar dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A strip of :class:ToolButton\ s with separators and a flexible spacer.

items is a list of ToolButton | "separator" | "spacer". Call :meth:layout (or :meth:paint, which lays out first) to assign each button's geometry; :meth:hit maps a point to a button. "spacer" pushes everything after it to the far edge.

docs.title: TabWidget dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A tab strip with content-width tabs plus a content panel that routes to the active tab. tabs is [(title, content), ...] where content is any object with x/y/w/h + paint(dl) (typically a Component) or None. Set closable for per-tab close buttons.

The host calls :meth:on_click to switch/close tabs, then :meth:paint, which lays the active content into :meth:content_rect and paints it.

docs.title: hit_tab(mx: float, my: float) -> tuple[str, int] | None
docs.text: Returns ("close", i) if a tab's close button was hit, else ("tab", i) for the tab body, else None.

docs.title: DockWidget dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A dockable panel: a title and a content (any object with x/y/w/h + paint). The :class:DockManager owns its placement; this type is mostly an identity + content holder. id must be unique within a manager (used for layout save/restore).

docs.title: DockManager dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Arranges :class:DockWidget\ s into docked areas around a central area (Qt's QMainWindow dock system).

Areas are "left" / "right" / "bottom" / "center". Each area is a tabbed region (multiple widgets share it via a tab strip). Splitter handles between the docked areas and the centre resize them. Dragging a tab shows drop-zone overlays and re-docks the widget on release. The whole layout serialises to/from a plain dict (wire to elysium.settings).

docs.title: tab_rects(area: str) -> list[tuple[int, float, float]]
docs.text: (index, x, width) for each tab in area.

docs.title: drop_zones() -> dict[str, tuple[float, float, float, float]]
docs.text: Edge/centre drop targets (for the drag overlay), keyed by area.

docs.title: restore(data: dict, registry: dict[str, DockWidget]) -> None
docs.text: Rebuild from :meth:serialize output. registry maps id → the live DockWidget (unknown ids are skipped).

docs.title: Drawer dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A panel that slides in from an edge over the area it covers (x/y/w/h = the overlaid region, usually the whole window). content is any object with x/y/w/h + paint. The host ticks :meth:update for the slide animation, paints it last, and routes clicks via :meth:on_click.

docs.title: Stepper dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A numbered step indicator. steps are titles; steps before current show a check, current is accented, later steps are muted.

docs.title: Wizard dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Component

A multi-step flow: a :class:Stepper header, the active step's content, and a Back/Next footer. steps are (title, content); content is any object with x/y/w/h + paint.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• App shell
  • Wizards and flows

docs.title: elysium.styling
docs.text: Declarative per-widget styling — a pragmatic, QSS-like StyleSheet/Selector layer over the theme tokens.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
Declarative per-widget styling — a pragmatic QSS-like layer over the theme.

A :class:StyleSheet maps selectors to property overrides. Selectors target a widget by type, #id, .class, and :state (hover / focus / pressed / disabled / checked), and combine — "Button.primary:hover". :meth:StyleSheet.resolve merges every matching rule in CSS-like specificity order into one property dict; :meth:apply writes those properties onto a widget instance.

This is not a full CSS engine — it's a selector→property resolver that layers on top of the existing token reads, so a widget stays theme-driven by default and only takes per-instance overrides where a rule matches.

docs.title: Selector dataclass
docs.text: A parsed selector. specificity orders matches: more specific wins (id ≫ class/state ≫ type), ties broken by source order.

docs.title: StyleSheet dataclass
docs.text: A collection of selector -> {property: value} rules. Pass a dict to the constructor; later rules of equal specificity win (source order).

docs.title: resolve(type_name: str, *, id: str | None = None, classes: Any = (), states: Any = ()) -> dict[str, Any]
docs.text: The merged property overrides for a widget. More-specific rules (and, on ties, later ones) win per property.

docs.title: apply(widget: Any, type_name: str | None = None, *, id: str | None = None, classes: Any = (), states: Any = ()) -> dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
Resolve and write the overrides onto widget (setattr for each property the widget already has). Returns the resolved dict.

type_name defaults to the widget's class name; id / classes default to the widget's style_id / style_classes if present.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • Styling and accessibility

docs.title: elysium.testing
docs.text: UiHarness — a QTest-equivalent headless driver over the real InputRouter.

docs.title: Auto-rendered details
docs.text:
UI test automation — Tier-2 Qt parity (QTest).

A headless driver for simulating user input against Elysium components and asserting on the resulting widget state — no window, GPU, or event loop. Built on the same :class:elysium.input.InputRouter apps use, so what the harness exercises is the real input path.

h = UiHarness([name_field, age_spin, table])
h.focus("name"); h.type("Ada")
assert h.find("name").value == "Ada"
h.click_widget("save")
assert "Saved" in h.texts()

type / key go through the router (focus nav, IME, clipboard, editing); click / scroll dispatch to the widget under the point the way an app's frame loop does. paint captures the display list for visual assertions.

docs.title: FakeWindow
docs.text: Minimal window stand-in implementing the surface the InputRouter + harness read from: a key queue, IME preedit, clipboard, scroll, cursor.

docs.title: CaptureDL
docs.text: A display-list stand-in that records drawn text and tallies calls; every other draw method is a no-op. Use texts for visual assertions.

docs.title: UiHarness
